articles.headline: SC stays planned CAG audit of power discoms in Capital
articles.summary: The Supreme Court stayed a proposed CAG audit of Delhi's three private power distribution companies. The court is examining the legality of the Delhi Electricity Regulatory Commission's decision to appoint the national auditor. The interim order is a setback for the Delhi government, which sought the audit to scrutinize finances before allowing recovery of over ₹38,500 crore in assets.

articles.headline: NGT orders probe in illegal mining near Sariska reserve
articles.summary: The National Green Tribunal ordered an independent inquiry into alleged illegalities in multiple mining leases near the Sariska Tiger Reserve in Rajasthan. The court heard a petition that several leases were delisted for failing to get mandatory clearances from the National Board of Wildlife. The probe will examine revised distance certificates that placed mines outside the protected zone.

articles.headline: Iran begins Khamenei's week-long funeral ceremonies in Tehran
articles.summary: Iran has begun a week of funeral processions for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who died at age 87. Dignitaries and foreign officials, including Indian representatives, paid respects at Tehran's Grand Mosalla. The event marks a critical moment for the nation as it enters a period of mourning and state transition amid regional tensions and international scrutiny.

articles.headline: SC questions bail for murder accused Sonam Raghuvanshi
articles.summary: The Supreme Court refused to stay the bail of Sonam Raghuvanshi, accused of murdering her husband during their honeymoon, but expressed reservations about the Meghalaya High Court order. The court issued notice to Raghuvanshi and the state government. The case involves a 'shocking' and 'pre-meditated' murder. The court will further hear the matter regarding her return to custody in July.

articles.headline: Yogesh Singh's term as DU Vice-Chancellor extended
articles.summary: President Droupadi Murmu has reappointed Yogesh Singh as Delhi University Vice-Chancellor, making him the first to serve consecutive terms in the institution's history. The reappointment follows 2023 statute amendments allowing a VC's second term. Singh was appointed the 23rd VC in 2021; his current term was set to end on October 7. He has overseen several key reforms in the university.

articles.headline: Centre to summon Meta over Instagram child abuse ads
articles.summary: The IT ministry will summon Meta officials following a BBC investigation alleging Instagram promoted ads featuring child sexual abuse material. Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw directed officials to seek an explanation on content moderation systems. The government's move comes amid growing concerns over child safety on social media. Meta stated they use automated tools to block such content.

articles.headline: Apple and Google told to remove e-rickshaw kill switch apps
articles.summary: The Centre has asked Apple and Google to remove at least seven mobile apps used to remotely disable battery-operated e-rickshaws. The apps, which connect via Bluetooth, were reportedly used by pranksters to switch off vehicles mid-journey. The action is taken under Section 69A of the IT Act. The ministry stated these apps pose a potential legal violation and risk to passenger safety.

articles.headline: Offside ball technology put to test in World Cup match
articles.summary: Controversy erupted as Croatia exited the World Cup after a stoppage-time equalizer was ruled out for offside using new motion-sensing ball technology. A 500Hz sensor inside the ball detects the exact moment of contact, while limb-tracking cameras map player positions. The VAR decision, based on a hair-width margin, has sparked debate over the use of extreme technology in the game.

articles.headline: Trump plans July 4 address at Mount Rushmore
articles.summary: President Donald Trump will kick off July 4th weekend with a speech at Mount Rushmore, South Dakota. The event will feature a massive fireworks display, which the National Park Service previously banned due to wildfire risks. Trump's speech is expected to focus on national pride and heritage. This trip is seen as part of his strategy to highlight historical monuments and American values.

articles.headline: US record fireworks show draws major health warnings
articles.summary: A project to launch more than 850,000 fireworks shells over the Potomac River has drawn health warnings from experts. The show, which aims to break the Guinness World Record, could significantly increase PM2.5 particulate matter levels. Analysts warn that the resulting smoke could create a localized air quality peak worse than typical levels seen in highly polluted Asian cities.

articles.headline: Manipur CM plans Churachandpur visit amid sporadic violence
articles.summary: Manipur Chief Minister N Biren Singh plans to visit Kuki-dominated Churachandpur on Saturday for the funeral of a Meitei MLA's relative. This would be the first visit by a Meitei representative to the district since ethnic clashes began in May 2023. Kuki-Zo groups have reportedly objected to the visit, citing a tense situation. Security reviews are underway to prevent any escalation.

articles.headline: El Nino to intensify by September setting off weather chaos
articles.summary: The WMO warns that El Nino conditions are likely to strengthen rapidly between July and September, raising the likelihood of heatwaves, droughts, and floods globally. Surface ocean temperatures in the Pacific are significantly above average. In India, El Nino is linked to weaker monsoons. Experts call for 'unprecedented mobilization' to mitigate impacts on food security and health.

articles.headline: Military purchases worth ₹52,000 crore cleared by DAC
articles.summary: The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by Rajnath Singh, approved the procurement of military hardware worth ₹52,000 crore. Acquisitions include 'AKASH TARANG' electronic warfare systems, anti-tank guided missiles, and medium-range surface-to-air missiles. The military hardware aims to boost the capabilities of the Indian Army, Navy, and Air Force amid regional security challenges.

articles.headline: India monitoring Bangladesh plans to develop Teesta with China
articles.summary: India is closely monitoring Bangladesh's plan to develop the Teesta River with Chinese assistance. Dhaka has unveiled plans for a $1 billion comprehensive management and restoration project. The move comes as New Delhi and Dhaka have long-standing unresolved issues regarding water sharing of the river. MEA stated that India will take 'appropriate measures' regarding its neighborhood interests.

articles.headline: Woman arrested over abuse in Bengaluru creche
articles.summary: A woman working at a daycare center on a corporate campus in Bengaluru was arrested for allegedly abusing a child. The incident came to light after a video surfaced showing the staffer mistreating the toddler. Police are investigating if other children were harmed and are searching for a second suspect. The daycare center's license is under review following the allegations.

articles.headline: RSS breaks silence on Ram Temple trust allegations
articles.summary: The Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS) has demanded a thorough investigation into alleged financial irregularities within the Ram Temple trust. General Secretary Dattatreya Hosabale stated that action must follow to protect the faith and reverence of the Hindu society. The RSS urged the government and the trust to show 'necessary patience' and ensure complete transparency in its operations.

articles.headline: Punjab Congress leaders seek Charanjit Channi as state president
articles.summary: Section of Punjab Congress leaders has urged the party high command to appoint former CM Charanjit Singh Channi as the state unit president. The demand follows a series of meetings among party leaders to rethink the leadership. Current PCC president Amarinder Singh Raja Warring is facing pressure amid speculation about organizational reshuffles. Channi is seen as a key Dalit face for the party.

articles.headline: KSEC holding parallel voter revision to please Rahul Gandhi
articles.summary: Union Minister Shobha Karandlaje alleged that the Karnataka State Election Commission (KSEC) is conducting a 'parallel' Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls to 'please Rahul Gandhi'. She claimed that officials are carrying out house-to-house verification in select areas under pressure from the state government. Karandlaje has requested the Chief Election Commissioner to intervene.

articles.headline: BJP seeks explanation from DK Shivakumar on Cauvery water
articles.summary: The BJP in Karnataka has sought an explanation from Deputy CM DK Shivakumar regarding the release of Cauvery water to Tamil Nadu despite a shortage in the state. BJP leaders alleged that water is being released from the Krishnaraja Sagar dam to 'please' alliance partners in the INDIA bloc. Shivakumar stated that water was released following standard protocols and legal obligations.

articles.headline: Three held in Bengaluru quarry collapse investigation
articles.summary: Three people, including the quarry owner, were arrested in connection with a stone quarry collapse in Bengaluru that killed seven workers. Preliminary investigations revealed that the quarry was operating without proper safety measures and mandatory licenses. The police are also probing operations at two adjoining sites. The state government has announced compensation for the victims' families.

articles.headline: Karnataka drafts new coastal tourism and cruise policy
articles.summary: The Karnataka government is drafting a 'Comprehensive Coastal Tourism Policy 2028' to unlock the state's tourism potential. The policy aims to develop world-class infrastructure in Dakshina Kannada, Udupi, and Uttara Kannada districts. Key features include the promotion of cruise tourism, beach resorts, and water sports. The government plans to invite global cruise operators to manage major circuits.

articles.headline: UP man arrested for hoax bomb threat to ISRO
articles.summary: A man from Ghaziabad was arrested for allegedly sending a hoax bomb threat email to the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) headquarters. The Delhi Police traced the email to the suspect, who reportedly committed the act under a false identity. No suspicious objects were found during the search of the premises. The accused has been booked under relevant sections of the BNS and IT Act.

articles.headline: DMK MLA arrested for public remarks against actor Vijay
articles.summary: DMK MLA Anitha R Radhakrishnan was arrested for making 'objectionable' comments against actor Vijay during a public meeting. The remarks reportedly provoked a strong reaction from fans and political opponents. The police booked the legislator under various sections for promoting enmity and inciting public disorder. The arrest follows a complaint filed by fans of the actor-turned-politician.

articles.headline: Kerala court stays AMMA ad hoc panel
articles.summary: The Ernakulam Additional Munsiff Court stayed the functioning of the ad hoc committee of the Association of Malayalam Movie Artists (AMMA). The court's decision follows a petition challenging the validity of the panel's formation. The committee was formed to manage the body's affairs after the entire executive committee resigned following the Hema Committee report on sexual harassment.

articles.headline: AIADMK exodus continues as former minister S Selvan resigns
articles.summary: Former AIADMK minister S Selvan has resigned from the party, marking a continued exodus of senior leaders. Selvan, who was a key figure in the party's southern stronghold, reportedly cited leadership failure and lack of inner-party democracy as reasons. His resignation comes amid a series of defections from the AIADMK to the DMK and other rival parties following recent electoral losses.

articles.headline: Hyderabad kamaans to be restored with 11.86 crore grant
articles.summary: The Telangana government has sanctioned ₹11.86 crore for the restoration of historical 'kamaans' (arches) in Hyderabad's Old City. The project aims to preserve the 400-year-old structures that were part of the original Qutb Shahi layout. The restoration will involve structural strengthening and artistic restoration to bring back their original glory. Work is expected to be completed within 18 months.

articles.headline: PM Modi to visit Indonesia, Australia and New Zealand
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will begin a three-nation tour on July 6, visiting Indonesia, Australia, and New Zealand. The visit aims to strengthen bilateral ties in trade, security, and defense. Key agendas include talks on the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement and meeting with the Indian diaspora. This will be the first visit by an Indian PM to New Zealand in over two decades.

articles.headline: India seeks info on missing seafarer from Venezuela
articles.summary: India has raised the issue of a missing seafarer with Venezuelan authorities. The merchant navy officer went missing from a vessel off the Venezuelan coast. The MEA stated it is in constant touch with the local government to fast-track the search and rescue operations. Family members have sought government intervention for a thorough investigation into the circumstances of his disappearance.

articles.headline: Places of Worship Act does not bar public-use takeover
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court ruled that the Places of Worship (Special Provisions) Act, 1991 does not prohibit the state from acquiring the property of a place of worship for a 'secular and public purpose'. The court dismissed a petition against the acquisition of land in Varanasi for a beautification project. It clarified that acquisition for infrastructure does not violate religious freedom.

articles.headline: M.P. to table draft UCC bill in cabinet meeting
articles.summary: The Madhya Pradesh government is set to table a draft bill on the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in the next cabinet meeting. The state government had formed a committee to study the implementation of UCC, similar to Uttarakhand. The draft includes recommendations on marriage, divorce, and inheritance laws. If approved, the bill will be introduced in the upcoming monsoon session of the assembly.

articles.headline: MHA and Ladakh bodies reach consensus after 3-hour meeting
articles.summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs and Ladakh-based organizations reached a consensus on several key demands after a three-hour meeting in Leh. The discussion focused on constitutional safeguards, land rights, and job protection for locals under the Sixth Schedule. Both parties agreed to form a high-powered committee to finalize the details of the agreement and ensure timely implementation.

articles.headline: India-Japan relations set for new era after Takaichi visit
articles.summary: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's visit to India has heralded a new era in bilateral relations. The two nations signed several agreements on defense technology, supply chain resilience, and clean energy. A key highlight was the joint declaration on the India-Japan AI Initiative. The visit also focused on strengthening maritime security in the Indo-Pacific region amid global challenges.

articles.headline: Russia and Ukraine exchange strikes after Kyiv drone attack
articles.summary: Russia and Ukraine exchanged heavy strikes following a major drone attack on Kyiv. At least 10 people were killed in Ukrainian strikes on Russian border regions, while Russian missiles targeted energy infrastructure across Ukraine. The exchange comes as both nations ramp up aerial warfare. International observers have expressed concern over the rising civilian toll and risk of further escalation.

articles.headline: NASA launches rescue mission for Swift space telescope
articles.summary: NASA has launched a rescue mission to save the Swift space telescope, which recently entered safe mode due to a technical glitch. The mission involves sending a repair signal and software updates to restore the telescope's ability to detect gamma-ray bursts. Swift has been a key tool in studying black holes and cosmic explosions since its launch in 2004. Officials hope to restore full operations.

articles.headline: HCL wins $1.14 billion Merc deal once held by Infosys
articles.summary: HCL Technologies has secured a $1.14 billion deal from Mercedes-Benz, a contract previously held by Infosys. The multi-year deal involves managing the German automaker's global digital workplace and cloud infrastructure. This win marks a significant expansion for HCL in the European automotive market. The transition is expected to be completed within the next six months.

articles.headline: India targets $1 trillion exports by FY27: Goyal
articles.summary: Union Minister Piyush Goyal stated that India is on track to achieve $1 trillion in merchandise exports by FY27. Speaking at a Board of Trade meeting, Goyal highlighted that the government is focusing on emerging sectors and new markets. He emphasized the importance of high-quality standards and competitive pricing to boost global trade presence. Current exports have shown steady growth despite global headwind.

articles.headline: Spain and Portugal prepare for World Cup quarter-final clash
articles.summary: Iberian rivals Spain and Portugal are set for a high-stakes quarter-final clash in the World Cup. Spain advanced after a dominant 3-0 win over Austria, while Portugal secured a narrow victory over Croatia. The match is expected to be a tactical battle between Spain's possession-based play and Portugal's clinical counter-attacks. Both teams have several star players in peak form heading into the game.

articles.headline: Novak Djokovic reaches Wimbledon Round of 16 with victory
articles.summary: Novak Djokovic moved into the Wimbledon Round of 16 after a four-set victory over Arthur Rinderknech. The top seed survived a scare in the second set to win 7-5, 6-4, 1-6, 7-6. This victory marks his 18th appearance in the fourth round at the All England Club. Djokovic will next face Russian qualifier Roman Safiullin. He remains the favorite to defend his title and equal Federer's record.

articles.headline: India Flips Switch for Beijing-linked Power Gear Cos
articles.summary: Four Indian firms, with local partners like L&T and Siemens, secured a 2-year exemption to supply critical power equipment. This bypasses a restrictive policy on Beijing-linked companies, ensuring continued infrastructure development and power grid stability.

articles.headline: Gulf of Gulf Sovereign Wealth Enters India in '26
articles.summary: Sovereign wealth funds (SWFs) from the Gulf region invested $1.75 billion in India during Q1 2024. Half of all SWF capital deployed in emerging markets went to India, indicating strong investor confidence and long-term strategic interest.

articles.headline: Meta Faces Child Abuse Content Glare, Summons
articles.summary: India's MeitY summoned Meta officials over alleged child sexual abuse material (CSAM) on its platforms. The ministry demands explanations for compliance with user safety guidelines, emphasizing the need for robust content moderation and user protection.

articles.headline: HCI, ITel Lacks in S1.14 b Mercedes-Benz Contract
articles.summary: HCI and ITel failed to secure a 5-year, $1.14 billion digital services contract from Mercedes-Benz. Instead, TCS and HCLTech are among the Indian companies that successfully bagged portions of the lucrative deal.

articles.headline: One Love Services for T20 WC
articles.summary: One Love Services, a BPO firm, was involved in providing support services for the T20 World Cup. This indicates its role in event logistics and operations, contributing to the smooth execution of the major sports event.

articles.headline: S'pore Firms to Invest $1.4 Billion
articles.summary: Singaporean firms have committed to investing $1.4 billion in various projects across India. This reinforces robust bilateral economic ties and is expected to boost infrastructure development and economic cooperation between the two nations.

articles.headline: D Street Ends Week with Lag of Gains
articles.summary: The Indian stock market closed the week with only marginal gains, reflecting cautious investor sentiment. The broader market experienced a lack of strong momentum, ending on a subdued note after a volatile trading period.

articles.headline: Adani Enterprises QIP Up to ₹12.5k Cr
articles.summary: Adani Enterprises plans to raise up to ₹12,500 crore via a Qualified Institutional Placement (QIP). The funds are intended to fuel the company's expansion plans and capital expenditure across various businesses.

articles.headline: ONDC Aims for 2.0% Profit Margin
articles.summary: The Open Network for Digital Commerce (ONDC) targets a 2.0% profit margin on its Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) within three years. This objective underscores its focus on sustainable growth and financial viability in the e-commerce sector.

articles.headline: India, Japan Agree on Crude Oil Stockpiling
articles.summary: India and Japan signed an agreement to cooperate on strategic crude oil stockpiling and reserve management. The initiative aims to enhance energy security, stabilize global markets, and facilitate information exchange on supply chain resilience and emergency response mechanisms.

articles.headline: India, Japan Pledges Cooperation on Digital Technologies
articles.summary: India and Japan reaffirmed their commitment to cooperate on digital technologies, including cybersecurity and ICT infrastructure. The collaboration aims to leverage technological advancements for mutual economic growth and security, fostering innovation and resilience.

articles.headline: Lessons from Op Sindoor: DAC Nod to Pseudo Satellites, Air Defense
articles.summary: The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) approved proposals for pseudo satellite technology and advanced air defense systems. These acquisitions, including drones and missiles, aim to modernize India's military capabilities and enhance national security, drawing lessons from Operation Sindoor.

articles.headline: Centre Extends Punjab Right to Business Act to Chandigarh
articles.summary: The Punjab Right to Business Act will be extended to Chandigarh, aiming to simplify regulatory processes for MSMEs. This move seeks to improve the ease of doing business, facilitate investments, and boost economic growth in the Union Territory.

articles.headline: Dissent Grows as Channi Camp Seeks Rethink on Warring
articles.summary: Dissent is growing within the Punjab Congress, with Sukhjinder Singh Randhawa and other leaders from the Channi camp demanding a review of state party chief Amrinder Singh Raja Warring's leadership following recent electoral setbacks.

articles.headline: CBI Nabs 3 in ₹2.07 cr Digital Arrest Scam
articles.summary: The CBI arrested three individuals for their alleged involvement in a ₹2.07 crore "digital arrest" scam. The fraudsters impersonated law enforcement officials to extort money from victims, highlighting a growing cybercrime trend targeting unsuspecting citizens.

articles.headline: Centre to Fund ₹52,000 cr Infra Projects
articles.summary: The Centre cleared 44 infrastructure projects worth ₹52,000 crore, primarily in the power and petroleum sectors. This significant investment aims to boost national infrastructure development, enhance energy security, and create employment opportunities across various regions.

articles.headline: Previous Meeting Held in Delhi Made Public
articles.summary: The proceedings of a significant political meeting held in Delhi were made public, emphasizing transparency in governance. This move aims to ensure public accountability and access to information regarding key policy discussions and decisions.

articles.headline: Nothing We Say May Influence Decision
articles.summary: A statement from the Opposition suggests that their views might not influence a pending government decision, highlighting a perceived lack of impact on policy outcomes. This reflects ongoing political dynamics and legislative challenges in the current scenario.

articles.headline: RSS Calls Ram Temple Donation Theft 'Unfortunate, Hurtful'
articles.summary: The RSS condemned the alleged theft of donations from the Ram Temple collection boxes, terming it "unfortunate and hurtful." The organization urged authorities to enhance security measures and ensure the sanctity and proper management of sacred donations.

articles.headline: HDK, BJP Cry Foul Over Sir, Allege Congress Interference
articles.summary: HD Kumaraswamy and the BJP accused the Congress government in Karnataka of interfering with the administration. They allege misuse of power and political vendettas, demanding fair governance and an end to partisan practices, impacting state politics.

articles.headline: CBSE Plans to Ease Norms for Private School Affiliation
articles.summary: CBSE is planning to relax affiliation norms for private schools, aiming to streamline the process and reduce bureaucracy. The move is expected to simplify compliance for new and existing schools, promoting better access to quality education across India.

articles.headline: Uddhav Alleges Temple Funds Used to Split Parties
articles.summary: Uddhav Thackeray alleged that funds collected for the Ram Temple were used by the BJP to split political parties in Maharashtra. He claimed that the temple construction was politicized to serve electoral agendas rather than religious devotion.

articles.headline: DMK MLA Arrested Over Remarks on Tamil Nadu CM
articles.summary: A DMK MLA has been arrested for making derogatory remarks against the Tamil Nadu Chief Minister. The arrest comes amid heightened political tensions and allegations of free speech suppression by the ruling party, sparking public debate.

articles.headline: Corrupt SDC Cleric Gets Adva From Mamata
articles.summary: A cleric accused of corruption received an advance from West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee, sparking controversy. This incident raises questions about accountability and political patronage within the state administration, leading to public scrutiny.

articles.headline: Krishna Byre Gowda Asks Agencies to Sync Infra Projects
articles.summary: Karnataka Minister Krishna Byre Gowda urged various agencies to synchronize infrastructure projects to avoid repeated digging and ensure efficient execution. This aims to minimize public inconvenience and optimize resource utilization for urban development, benefiting citizens.

articles.headline: US' Holtec Eyes Tie-ups With NTPC, Vedanta
articles.summary: US-based Holtec International is exploring partnerships with NTPC and Vedanta to develop modular nuclear reactors (SMRs) in India. This collaboration aims to leverage advanced nuclear technology for clean energy generation and energy security, fostering sustainable development.

articles.headline: Apps Blocked Over EV Shutdowns Due to Auto Cybersecurity
articles.summary: Several mobile apps are being blocked on electric vehicles following cybersecurity concerns. This measure aims to protect user data and vehicle systems from potential threats, though it impacts the functionality and user experience of smart cars.

articles.headline: Eye on 17% Goods Exports Growth Next Fiscal
articles.summary: India aims for a 17% growth in goods exports in the next fiscal year, targeting key markets like the EU, UK, and Africa. The government plans to boost exports through free trade agreements and diversification into new product categories.

articles.headline: Middle Class to Remain India's Growth Engine
articles.summary: The finance ministry emphasized that the middle class will continue to be the primary engine of India's economic growth. Their rising consumption and investment capabilities are crucial for driving demand and sustaining the nation's development trajectory, supporting overall prosperity.

articles.headline: Monsoon Watch: Rainfall Up
articles.summary: Rainfall distribution across India indicates an increase in precipitation, crucial for agriculture and water resources. The monsoon forecast suggests continued active rainfall, impacting cropping patterns, reservoir levels, and overall water availability for the upcoming season.

articles.headline: World Cup Fails to Score For TV Makers
articles.summary: TV manufacturers saw a sales dip in June, partly attributed to lower-than-expected viewership during the T20 World Cup. Despite the tournament, demand for new televisions did not surge as anticipated, impacting retail sales and the consumer electronics market.

articles.headline: As Food Inflation Bites, Nestle Turns to Smaller Cities to Drive Demand
articles.summary: Nestle is focusing on smaller cities and towns to drive demand amidst rising food inflation. The strategy involves targeting new consumption pockets and offering value-for-money products to sustain growth in challenging economic conditions, expanding its market reach.

articles.headline: Limited Audience Impact Undercuts Zee5
articles.summary: Zee5's limited audience impact and challenges in securing key content rights have led to significant financial setbacks, resulting in widespread layoffs. The company is facing increased competition and pressure to achieve profitability in a competitive streaming market.

articles.headline: Telcos Do Well in Q1, But Tariff Hikes Likely
articles.summary: Indian telecom companies performed strongly in Q1, driven by increased subscriber bases and data consumption. However, analysts anticipate tariff hikes in the second half of the year to further boost Average Revenue Per User (ARPU) and revenue growth.

articles.headline: ACME Unit to Supply $10 bn Green Ammonia
articles.summary: ACME Group's unit will supply $10 billion worth of green ammonia to customers, emphasizing its role in the renewable energy sector. This initiative underscores India's push towards sustainable energy solutions and a greener economy, attracting global partners.

articles.headline: Screenwriters Wanted to Create $2.65-B Content
articles.summary: India aims to generate $2.65 billion worth of content through a talent hunt for screenwriters. This initiative seeks to boost the creative economy, foster original storytelling, and provide opportunities for emerging and established writers in the entertainment industry.

articles.headline: 'Pre-crime' Ain't a Policing Option
articles.summary: The editorial argues against "pre-crime" policing tactics, emphasizing that AI and data analytics should not be used to predict future crimes or target individuals before offenses occur. It advocates for ethical use of technology in law enforcement to avoid biases.

articles.headline: Open and Shut Big AI Overselling Case
articles.summary: The article criticizes the overhyping of AI capabilities, stating that many claims about advanced AI are speculative. It argues that a significant portion of AI's current impact is due to basic automation, not sentient intelligence, urging a more realistic perspective.

articles.headline: The Unconditional Love
articles.summary: This piece explores the concept of unconditional love, particularly in the context of human relationships and personal growth. It delves into the emotional depth and psychological impact of selfless affection, highlighting its significance in well-being.

articles.headline: 250 Years of Trysts With the Future
articles.summary: This opinion piece reflects on 250 years of global historical progress and the concept of "trysts with the future." It examines how nations and individuals have shaped their destiny through choices and challenges over centuries, exploring societal evolution.

articles.headline: What America (Still) Teaches Us
articles.summary: This article discusses the enduring lessons that America offers the world, from innovation and democracy to economic models. It explores both the strengths and weaknesses, providing a nuanced view of its global influence and historical contributions.

articles.headline: Sleeping Your Way to Success
articles.summary: This article emphasizes the critical role of adequate sleep for success, productivity, and overall well-being. It highlights how sufficient rest improves cognitive function, decision-making, and emotional resilience, advocating for prioritizing sleep as a strategic asset.

articles.headline: Flight of Capital on Saturday
articles.summary: This brief discusses the phenomenon of capital flight occurring on weekends, likely due to financial markets closing. It indicates strategies investors might use to move funds during non-trading hours, potentially impacting market stability.

articles.headline: Drill, Baby, Drill, For Oil
articles.summary: This brief discusses the ongoing debate surrounding increased oil drilling. It likely addresses the economic benefits of expanded fossil fuel extraction versus environmental concerns, highlighting policy implications and industry discussions.

articles.headline: Who's Watching Our Privacy?
articles.summary: This brief raises concerns about personal privacy in the digital age, questioning who has access to our data and how it is used. It touches upon issues of surveillance, data breaches, and the importance of robust data protection measures.

articles.headline: Tough Rules Shatter NRIs' Equity Dreams
articles.summary: Stricter rules and taxation policies are dampening the enthusiasm of Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) for investing in Indian equities. These regulatory hurdles are making it more challenging for NRIs to participate effectively in the stock market, impacting foreign investment.

articles.headline: Udan Flights Discontinued on 50% of the Routes
articles.summary: Approximately 50% of the routes under the UDAN regional connectivity scheme have been discontinued due to financial unviability and lack of demand. This impacts air travel in smaller towns, raising questions about the scheme's long-term effectiveness.

articles.headline: NuVentures Invests in US-based Firm LinqAlpha
articles.summary: NuVentures, an Indian venture capital firm, has invested in US-based LinqAlpha, an AI-driven marketing technology company. This strategic investment aims to bolster LinqAlpha's growth and expand its market reach, fostering innovation in marketing tech.

articles.headline: General Insurance: Call for CXO Executive
articles.summary: Universal Sompo General Insurance is seeking a CXO-level executive for a leadership role. The position involves strategic management, driving business growth, and enhancing customer operations within the general insurance sector, focusing on experienced candidates.

articles.headline: D-St Also Smokes Peace Pipe, Ends in Green
articles.summary: Indian equities ended the week in the green, with key indices showing positive movement. Despite earlier volatility, a late surge in buying across sectors helped the market close with gains, reflecting renewed investor confidence.

articles.headline: Rupee Ends Losing Run on Whiff of FPI Inflows
articles.summary: The Indian rupee strengthened against the dollar, ending its losing streak due to expectations of fresh foreign portfolio investment (FPI) inflows. This positive sentiment is driven by improving economic outlook and global market trends, bolstering currency stability.

articles.headline: Sebi Tweaks Rules on Handling of Unpaid Securities
articles.summary: SEBI has modified rules for handling unpaid securities and funds in trading accounts. The changes aim to streamline the process, enhance investor protection, and ensure greater transparency and efficiency in the financial markets, benefiting investors.

articles.headline: Bond Street, Falling Yields Spur ₹3,700-cr Fundraise
articles.summary: Indian companies raised ₹3,700 crore through bond issuances, driven by falling yields and favorable market conditions. This trend indicates a robust debt market, supporting corporate financing and investment activities across various sectors, boosting economic growth.

articles.headline: SBI Funds Seeks ₹2K cr in Pre-IPO Placement
articles.summary: SBI Funds Management is planning to raise ₹2,000 crore through a pre-IPO placement. This strategic move aims to secure early investments ahead of its initial public offering, positioning the fund for future growth and expanding its market presence.

articles.headline: Zero Tolerance to CSAM, Says Meta
articles.summary: Meta reiterated its "zero tolerance" policy for Child Sexual Abuse Material (CSAM), emphasizing its continuous efforts to combat such content through advanced technology and collaboration with law enforcement agencies. This highlights their commitment to user safety.

articles.headline: Adani Ent QIP Gets Nearly 4X Bids
articles.summary: Adani Enterprises' Qualified Institutional Placement (QIP) received bids nearly four times the offer size, indicating strong investor confidence. The oversubscription suggests robust demand for the company's shares and future growth prospects, boosting market sentiment.

articles.headline: Kpack Packaging IPO Gets 83X Bids
articles.summary: Kpack Packaging's Initial Public Offering (IPO) was oversubscribed by 83 times, demonstrating overwhelming investor demand. The strong response reflects positive market sentiment towards the packaging sector and the company's growth potential, securing significant capital.

articles.headline: AI-led Operating Model Huge Revenue
articles.summary: Companies implementing AI-led operating models are experiencing huge revenue growth, with some seeing annual revenue for artificial intelligence growing 5.5x faster than companies not using it. This trend highlights AI's transformative impact on business efficiency and profitability.

articles.headline: Transmission Plans for Power
articles.summary: India is developing extensive transmission plans to enhance its power grid infrastructure, focusing on improving connectivity and reliability. These initiatives are crucial for integrating renewable energy and meeting growing electricity demand across the nation, ensuring energy security.

articles.headline: Portugal Survive a Chaotic Finish to Reach Last 16
articles.summary: Portugal narrowly escaped a chaotic finish against Hungary to secure a 2-1 victory, advancing to the Euro 2024 Round of 16. The match saw late drama and controversial VAR decisions, ultimately ensuring their progression in the tournament.

articles.headline: France, Beware: Benzema Threatens Neymar
articles.summary: Karim Benzema issued a warning to Neymar, emphasizing that France is ready to face Brazil in an upcoming match. The rivalry between the players and nations adds intensity to the contest, setting the stage for a thrilling football encounter.

articles.headline: Hamilton Puts Ferrari on Pole for Brazilian GP
articles.summary: Lewis Hamilton secured pole position for Ferrari at the Brazilian Grand Prix, showcasing strong performance in qualifying. This puts him in a prime position to challenge for victory, setting up an exciting race weekend for Formula 1 enthusiasts.

articles.headline: Mahakala in Baghdad: How Tantra Shaped Islam
articles.summary: This article explores the historical influence of Tantric traditions, particularly the deity Mahakala, on early Islamic esoteric practices in Baghdad. It delves into cultural exchange and the synthesis of religious thought, highlighting cross-cultural intellectual connections.

articles.headline: Should India Aspire to be a DIY Society?
articles.summary: This opinion piece questions whether India should strive to become a "DIY society" (Do It Yourself). It explores the implications of self-reliance, local innovation, and domestic production on economic growth and societal development, fostering a national debate.

articles.headline: SIT widens scrutiny into Ram Temple trust financials
articles.summary: A three-member Special Investigation Team (SIT) has expanded its probe into the financial system of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust. The team is scrutinizing the complete trail of donations received from within India and abroad during the temple's construction. This follows allegations of financial irregularities and 'unauthorized' fund management within the trust.

articles.headline: DMK moves SC in Karur stampede case for CBI probe
articles.summary: The DMK has moved the Supreme Court seeking a CBI probe into the Karur stampede incident. The party alleged that the state government's distribution of gift coupons led to the crowd surge that killed several people. The DMK claimed that the current police investigation is compromised and lacks transparency. The petition seeks to ensure accountability of officials and fair compensation for victims.

articles.headline: Services sector growth hits 17-month low in June
articles.summary: India's services sector growth slowed to a 17-month low in June, as measured by the HSBC Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI). The index fell to 57.4 from 61.2 in May, cited as a result of weak domestic demand and inflationary pressures. Despite the decline, the sector remains in expansion territory (above 50). Experts suggest that business confidence remains positive for the long-term outlook.

articles.headline: England to face Australia in women's T20 World Cup final
articles.summary: England will take on arch-rivals Australia in the final of the women's T20 World Cup. England secured their place with a dominant 40-run win over South Africa in the semi-final, powered by Nat Sciver-Brunt's half-century. Australia, the defending champions, are seeking their seventh title. The final is scheduled to be held at Lord's on Sunday, with a record crowd expected for the showpiece event.
